blockers.description: The searchForScheduled interface accepts handleShowModal(ownProps: unknown), but the exact callback key within ownProps that the modal host uses to restore focus after dismissal is not specified.
blockers.resolution: Pass { isMessage, onCloseCustomAction: () => setContainFocus?.(true) } to handleShowModal and call setContainFocus?.(false) immediately before it. The required restoration key is onCloseCustomAction — do NOT use onClose, afterClose, or any other key.
example_questions: -  What property key should be passed inside ownProps to handleShowModal for restoring focus after the scheduled-items modal is dismissed?
-  Which callback name does the modal host contract expect for the focus-restoration action in searchForScheduled?
-  Should the focus-restoration callback be named onClose, onCloseCustomAction, afterClose, or something else when calling handleShowModal?
-  What is the expected shape of the ownProps object passed to handleShowModal in the scheduled-items flow?
-  What contract does the modal host impose on the ownProps payload for lifecycle callbacks like focus restoration after dismiss?

blockers.description: The requirements say getNotificationTextMoved should generate text for moves originating from Spam, but do not specify whether this applies to all destination folders or excludes Trash.
blockers.resolution: getNotificationTextMoved must check both fromLabelID === SPAM AND folderID !== TRASH for the "not spam list" notification. Moving from Spam to Trash is a deletion that should produce the default "moved to Trash" text; only non-Trash destinations get the "not spam" notification.
example_questions: -  When moving messages from Spam to Trash, should the notification say "added to your not spam list" or just "moved to Trash"?
-  Does the "not spam list" notification text apply to all moves originating from Spam, or only non-Trash destinations?
-  Should getNotificationTextMoved treat Spam-to-Trash differently from Spam-to-Inbox or Spam-to-Archive?
-  Is moving from Spam to Trash semantically an un-spam action or a deletion?
-  What destination folder restriction, if any, should the from-Spam notification text have?

blockers.description: The problem statement says scheduled-move handling applies "when moving exclusively scheduled items to Trash", but the requirements say searchForScheduled should evaluate scheduled-selection outcomes "for the move path being handled", suggesting all destinations.
blockers.resolution: searchForScheduled must only evaluate scheduled items when folderID === TRASH; for all other move destinations, the function returns immediately without calling setCanUndo or showing a modal.
example_questions: -  Should searchForScheduled evaluate scheduled items only for Trash moves, or for all destination folders?
-  If the destination is Inbox or Archive, should searchForScheduled still count scheduled items and potentially disable undo?
-  Does the scheduled-evaluation logic apply exclusively to Trash, or to every move path the hook handles?
-  When moving scheduled messages to a non-Trash folder, should searchForScheduled return immediately without any side effects?
-  Is the folderID === TRASH guard required at the top of searchForScheduled, or should it run unconditionally?
